title: Rose water face toner
subtitle: A simple hydrating toner spray made from rose water and distilled water
excerpt: A two-ingredient facial toner made from rose water and distilled water, decanted into a small spray bottle. Applied after cleansing, it adds a layer of hydration before moisturiser. Without a preservative, it keeps for 30 days in the fridge. Make a fresh bottle each month.
sourceNotes: Formulators Kitchen hydrosol toner guidance (formulatorskitchen.com, accessed May 2026): preservative-free hydrosol shelf life and refrigeration requirements. Rose water as a hydrosol skin toner: standard practice in Middle Eastern and South Asian beauty routines.

content.text: used as a skin toner in Middle Eastern, South Asian, and North African beauty traditions for many centuries. Used after cleansing and before moisturiser, it adds a layer of hydration and leaves the skin feeling fresh. This recipe softens shop rose water with a little distilled water, so the floral scent is gentler. The toning effect stays the same.
Because this toner is water-based and contains no

, it must be refrigerated and used within 30 days. Make a small bottle each month rather than a large quantity. Always check the toner smells fresh before applying to the face. If it smells fermented or off, discard it immediately.

items.name: Rose water
items.prepNote: Commercial rose water or a genuine hydrosol. Check it contains no artificial fragrance additives.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: Dilutes the rose water slightly. Do not use tap water; its mineral content and microbiological load will shorten the shelf life.

content.text: Weigh the rose water and distilled water into a small clean jug. Mix briefly. Using the small funnel, pour into the amber glass bottle and fit the spray top. Label with the making date and the discard date (30 days from making). Refrigerate immediately.

After cleansing, mist the face from 20 cm away. Allow the toner to absorb for 30 seconds before applying moisturiser or serum. The slight floral scent will be present immediately after application and fades as the skin dries. Keep the bottle in the bathroom but return to the fridge between uses if possible.

Refrigerate. Use within 30 days of making and discard at the end of that period regardless of appearance. If the toner looks cloudy, smells fermented or off, or causes any stinging on application before the 30-day mark, discard it immediately and clean the bottle thoroughly before making a fresh batch.
